goal: Implement the Blueprint FTC MVP per context.md (product writeup from writeup.pdf). Blueprint is an AI engineering workspace for FIRST Tech Challenge teams that turns the official game manual, team constraints, budget, inventory, strategy, and preferred supplier into a complete first-robot planning package: strategy, architecture, legal checklist, REV-first BOM, budget, physics calculations, conceptual CAD, FTC SDK Java starter code, build guide, grant support, chatbot iteration, and driver-control optimization. The full product spec, current implementation status, recommended milestones, acceptance criteria, and risks live in `context.md` at the repo root. Read it. Honor it. Build to the acceptance criteria. Workers MUST treat context.md as the contract.
summary: Blueprint FTC MVP: workspace wizard, full backend (RAG/catalog/physics/CAD/code-validation/grants/driver-logs), integration, verifier
acceptanceCriteria: A team can create a profile via a real wizard flow in the UI
A team can upload the current FTC manual PDF and see citations preserved with page + rule numbers
A team can enter budget, inventory, tools, skill level, timeline, priorities, and supplier
Blueprint generates three robot concepts, the team selects one, and a full BOM with estimated cost appears
Mechanism math (torque/RPM/lift/arm/current/COG/tipping) is shown with assumptions, formulas, results, safety margins
A basic CAD layout renders in the browser and exports a downloadable artifact
Starter FTC SDK Java code is downloadable and aligns with the hardware configuration guide
A build guide, chatbot tab, sponsor email generator, and driver-log upload+analysis flow are all reachable from the workspace
`npm run build` and `npm run lint` pass on the integrated branch

tasks.name: backend-mvp
tasks.scopedGoal:
Complete the BACKEND half of the Blueprint FTC MVP per `context.md` (repo root). Read `context.md` fully before editing. Owns everything under `server/**`. Do NOT touch `src/**`, `index.html`, or `public/**` — the frontend worker owns those.

Deliver (each item below maps to context.md milestones 2-5 plus partial items):

## Manual ingestion (Milestone 2)
- Improve `chunkText`/`parsePdf` so chunks carry a real `page` number (use `pdf-parse` per-page text, not the concatenated string).
- Improve section-heading detection (multi-line headings, numeric section ids like `R101`, `<R703>`, `10.4.3`).
- Preserve rule numbers (R1xx, T1xx, RG1xx, GS1xx) on chunks; surface them in `/api/projects/:id/rules/search` results with page + section + rule.
- Add `version` + `checksum` to documents (checksum already partially present) and expose `lastChecked`.
- Add a documents `list` endpoint that includes per-document page counts and version.
- Add refusal helper: when `chat`/`rules/search` cannot find a citation, the response MUST surface `confidence: 'low'` and a refusal note instead of inventing legality claims.

## REV catalog (Milestone 3)
- Expand seed URLs + REV search discovery; persist the catalog cache to `.cache/rev-catalog.json` (existing path).
- Normalize categories (Motors, Servos, Wheels, Power transmission, Structure, Linear motion, Control system, Electrical, Pneumatics, Other) with a deterministic mapping function.
- Capture mechanical metadata (weight, dimensions, material) when present in BCData/JSON-LD.
- Capture electrical metadata (voltage, current, signal type) when present.
- Add manual price override endpoint `POST /api/catalog/products/:sku/override { price?, stock?, notes? }` that persists to `.cache/rev-catalog.json`.
- Add compatibility/required-accessory metadata fields (best-effort from product copy/related-products) and surface them in `/api/catalog/products/:sku`.

## Model adapter (Milestone 4)
- Extract the Vertex call into a provider-neutral adapter module at `server/lib/model-adapter.js` exporting `callJson({ provider, prompt, model, schema })` with `provider: 'vertex' | 'openai' | 'anthropic' | 'local-fallback'`. Implement Vertex (existing); stub OpenAI/Anthropic with a clean fetch shape so adding keys is a 5-line change.
- Add Review-Agent gate: `POST /api/projects/:id/agents/review-plan` actually runs through the adapter (when configured) and refuses to bless a plan that has uncited legality claims, missing safety factors, or missing hardware-name consistency. When the adapter is unavailable, still run deterministic review checks and return them.
- Add prompt/version audit trail: `state.projects.get(id).auditTrail = []` accumulating `{ ts, agent, model, promptHash, responseHash }` rows; expose `GET /api/projects/:id/audit`.
- Store generated artifacts per project on disk under `.cache/projects/<id>/` so a restart preserves the last generation.

## Physics expansion (context.md MVP Calculations list)
Add to `calculateMechanisms` (or its module) and `/api/projects/:id/calculate/mechanism`:
- Current draw estimate (per motor; sum across drivetrain)
- Battery load estimate (12 V REV battery, percent of recommended 20 A continuous)
- Center of gravity estimate (given subsystem masses + heights)
- Tipping risk estimate (lateral acceleration vs. wheelbase)
- Chain/belt speed
- Pulley ratio tuning
- Servo torque margin (REV smart servo, GoBilda servo as references)
- Intake roller speed
- Linear slide load model with stage friction
Each calc returns the same shape currently used: `{ mechanism, formula, inputs, result, recommendation, safetyFactor }` and includes a warning string when margin < 1.25.

## Code validation (Milestone 5)
- Add a minimal FTC SDK fixture under `server/fixtures/ftc-sdk-stub/` containing just enough Java stubs (`OpMode`, `LinearOpMode`, `Telemetry`, `HardwareMap`, `DcMotor`, `DcMotorEx`, `Servo`, `CRServo`, `IMU`, `BNO055IMU`, `@TeleOp`, `@Autonomous`) to make `javac` happy on the generated TeamCode files. No Gradle; plain `javac` invocation against the stub jar/sources is enough.
- Add `POST /api/projects/:id/code/validate` that writes the current `project.code` files to a temp dir, runs `javac` against the stub sources, and returns `{ ok, errors: [{ file, line, message }], stdout, stderr, durationMs }`.
- Auto-validate after every `generate-code` and stamp `project.codeValidation` on success/failure.
- Document the fixture in `server/fixtures/ftc-sdk-stub/README.md`.

## Driver-log analysis (context.md Driver-Control Optimization)
- Accept CSV in addition to JSON at `POST /api/projects/:id/driver-logs/analyze`. Schema: `timestamp,button,value,phase,robotState` (header-driven, tolerant of column reorder).
- Add match-phase analysis (autonomous vs teleop vs endgame split based on timestamp/phase column).
- Add timing-gap report (median + p95 gap between repeated actions).
- Add control heatmap (button × phase frequency matrix).
- Add robot-state correlation (button-press → state-change windows).
- Return all of the above in the existing response shape, additively (do not break existing consumers).

## Grants / Sponsor CRM (context.md Grant And Sponsor Assistant)
- Add `POST /api/teams/:id/sponsors` (create), `GET /api/teams/:id/sponsors`, `PATCH /api/teams/:id/sponsors/:sponsorId` (status, follow-up date, notes), `DELETE /api/teams/:id/sponsors/:sponsorId`. Persist on `project.sponsors[]` and to disk under `.cache/projects/<id>/sponsors.json`.
- Add `POST /api/teams/:id/follow-up-email` that drafts a follow-up using the existing sponsor template.
- Add `POST /api/teams/:id/thank-you-email`.
- Add `GET /api/teams/:id/sponsor-packet.html` (one-page sponsorship packet HTML, downloadable like build-guide export).

## Engineering hygiene
- Add a `server/lib/` directory; extract big concerns (`rag.js`, `catalog.js`, `physics.js`, `cad.js`, `code-gen.js`, `code-validate.js`, `driver-logs.js`, `grants.js`, `model-adapter.js`). `server/index.js` becomes a thin Express wiring file that requires these modules. Keep API behavior backwards-compatible; only refactor, do not rename routes that already exist.
- Add minimal Node-builtin `node:test` tests under `server/__tests__/` for: physics calc shape, catalog category normalization, driver-log CSV parsing, refusal behavior. Wire a `npm run test:server` script in `package.json` that runs `node --test server/__tests__/`.

## Out of scope (do NOT do)
- Anything in `src/**`, `index.html`, `public/**` (frontend worker owns this).
- Renaming any existing route paths.
- Hosted-LLM dependencies that require runtime API keys (Vertex stays optional via env).
- Pushing or opening a PR — the merge worker handles delivery. Just commit to your task branch.

## API contract you MUST honor (frontend worker reads the same contract)
Do not change these shapes; only add fields:
- `GET /api/project/demo` → `ApiProjectResponse` (see `src/types.ts`).
- `POST /api/projects/:id/generate-blueprint` → `ApiProjectResponse`.
- `POST /api/projects/:id/documents/upload` → `{ document, project }`.
- `POST /api/catalog/sync` → `{ synced, products, errors }`.
- `POST /api/projects/:id/chat` → `{ answer, citations, catalogHits, generatedBy, suggestedActions, confidence?, refusal? }` (the last two are additive).
- `POST /api/projects/:id/driver-logs/analyze` → `{ summary, suggestions, phaseSplit?, timingGaps?, heatmap?, robotStateCorrelation? }`.
- Sponsor CRM endpoints listed above are NEW.

## Acceptance you self-check before handoff
- `npm run build` passes (frontend build is fine to skip if frontend is unchanged on your branch; if it breaks because you altered the API contract in a breaking way, fix it).
- `node --test server/__tests__/` passes.
- `node server/index.js` boots; `curl localhost:8787/api/health` returns 200 with non-zero `catalogItems` after a sync.
- All endpoints listed above respond to a smoke `curl` (POST with `Content-Type: application/json -d '{}'` is enough).

Handoff branch: `orch/blueprint-mvp/backend-mvp`. Do not push to any other branch.
acceptance: Manual ingestion preserves page numbers and rule numbers on chunks
REV catalog has category normalization, mechanical/electrical metadata, manual price overrides, and compatibility fields
Provider-neutral model adapter at server/lib/model-adapter.js with vertex/openai/anthropic/local-fallback providers
Review-Agent endpoint runs deterministic checks even without LLM keys and refuses uncited legality claims
Project audit trail recorded and exposed at GET /api/projects/:id/audit
Physics endpoint returns at least current-draw, battery-load, COG, tipping, chain/belt, pulley, servo-margin, roller, slide calcs in addition to the existing four
Sample FTC SDK stub fixture at server/fixtures/ftc-sdk-stub/ and POST /api/projects/:id/code/validate runs javac against the stubs
Driver-log analyze endpoint accepts CSV and returns phase split, timing gaps, heatmap, and state correlation
Sponsor CRM endpoints (create/list/patch/delete + follow-up + thank-you + packet HTML) all respond
server/lib/ modules extracted; server/index.js wires them; existing routes unchanged in path
node --test server/__tests__/ passes; npm run build passes
Branch orch/blueprint-mvp/backend-mvp pushed

## Automated

tasks.name: frontend-mvp
tasks.scopedGoal:
Complete the FRONTEND half of the Blueprint FTC MVP per `context.md` (repo root). Read `context.md` fully before editing. Owns everything under `src/**`, `index.html`, and `public/**`. Do NOT touch `server/**` — the backend worker owns those.

Deliver (each item below maps to context.md Milestone 1 + partial frontend items):

## Project wizard (Milestone 1, primary)
- Add a real multi-step project wizard reachable from the landing page. Steps:
  1. Team profile (name, number, location, experience level, students, mentors).
  2. Build context (tools available, build space, CAD experience, programming experience).
  3. Budget and inventory (budget number, supplier dropdown defaulting to REV Robotics, free-text inventory list).
  4. Timeline + priorities (competition timeline in weeks, multi-select robot priorities from context.md list: Low cost, Easy to build, Fast drivetrain, Strong endgame, Reliable autonomous, Simple driver control, Easy maintenance, Maximum scoring potential, Alliance-friendly reliability).
  5. Strategy mode picker (AI-generated, team-provided, hybrid — radio buttons).
  6. Review + submit → calls `POST /api/projects` (or `PATCH /api/projects/:id` for an existing project) and then `POST /api/projects/:id/generate-blueprint`.
- Wizard state lives in a React hook (`useProjectWizard`) and persists locally via `localStorage` so a refresh mid-flow restores progress.
- The wizard MUST validate required fields before allowing 'next' and surface inline errors.
- After submit, the wizard routes the user into the Workspace with the freshly generated project loaded.

## Upload UIs (context.md partial items)
- Add an Inventory upload (CSV or paste) panel on the Dashboard tab. Parser accepts headers `part,sku,qty,notes` (tolerant of reorder).
- Add a Driver-log upload (CSV or JSON) panel on a new `Driver Logs` workspace tab (or under the Chat tab; pick one and commit). On upload, call `POST /api/projects/:id/driver-logs/analyze`, render phase split, timing gaps, heatmap (simple table), state correlation, and existing suggestion list.
- Keep the existing PDF upload UI working for the season manual.

## Editable artifacts (context.md partial items)
- BOM tab gains row-level edit: change qty, override price, mark as already-owned (checkbox). Persist via `POST /api/projects/:id/generate-bom` for full regenerate and `PATCH /api/projects/:id` for granular edits. (If the backend exposes per-item PATCH, use it; otherwise round-trip the whole bom array in PATCH body.)
- Design tab gains a 'merge concepts' button that calls the existing select-design endpoint with a synthesized concept name.
- Strategy tab gains editable bullet list backed by `project.strategy` (whatever shape the backend returns; render strings as-is).
- Add a 'substitutions' UI on the BOM tab: when a BOM row has substitution candidates from the catalog, show them as an inline picker.

## Sponsor CRM UI (context.md Grant And Sponsor Assistant)
- Add a `Grants` workspace tab.
- Add 'Generate sponsor email' button that calls `POST /api/teams/:teamId/sponsor-email` and shows the result in a copy-friendly textarea.
- Add 'Generate grant draft' button calling `POST /api/teams/:teamId/grant-draft`.
- Add a Sponsor CRM table backed by `GET /api/teams/:teamId/sponsors`: columns Organization, Contact, Status, Amount requested, Amount received, Follow-up date, Notes; add/edit/delete inline (calls the CRM endpoints listed in the backend contract).
- Add 'Download sponsor packet' linking `GET /api/teams/:teamId/sponsor-packet.html`.

## Apply-chat-suggestion workflow (context.md MVP feature)
- The Chat tab response from the API includes `suggestedActions: string[]`. Render each as a clickable chip. Clicking a known action ('regenerate BOM', 'recalculate mechanism', 'update code artifact', 'search rules') invokes the corresponding existing API. Unknown actions stay disabled with a tooltip.

## Responsive workspace + polish
- Make the Workspace shell responsive: sidebar collapses below 1024 px, tab strip becomes horizontally scrollable below 768 px.
- Add a top-of-workspace 'AI status' badge that uses `aiStatus` to show provider/model and switches color when `ready: false`.
- Honor `confidence` and `refusal` fields when present on chat answers — show refusal text in a callout block instead of inline.

## Code & build guide tabs
- Code tab: add a `Validate` button calling `POST /api/projects/:id/code/validate`; render `{ ok, errors: [{ file, line, message }] }` in a compact error list.
- Build tab: turn build guide steps into the LEGO-style format described in context.md (number, title, parts, tools, time, instructions, checkpoint, common mistake, test). Backend already returns `BuildGuideStep`; flesh out the UI to render all fields.

## API contract you MUST honor (backend worker reads the same contract)
- See the backend worker's contract section, which is the source of truth.
- Any NEW fields (`confidence`, `refusal`, sponsor CRM endpoints, driver-log phaseSplit/timingGaps/heatmap/robotStateCorrelation, code validate response) MAY arrive — render them when present, ignore when absent.

## Engineering hygiene
- Keep components functional + typed. Use `src/types.ts` for new shapes (extend, don't break).
- Move large new components into their own files (`src/wizard/`, `src/grants/`, `src/driverlogs/`).
- `npm run lint` and `npm run build` MUST pass on your branch.
- No new heavy dependencies. Lucide icons + native form controls + plain CSS modules are sufficient.

## Out of scope (do NOT do)
- Anything in `server/**`.
- Adding authentication, persistent DB, or background job queue (context.md marks them as not-MVP).
- Pushing or opening a PR — the merge worker handles delivery. Just commit to your task branch.

Handoff branch: `orch/blueprint-mvp/frontend-mvp`. Do not push to any other branch.
acceptance: Real multi-step project wizard exists with 6 steps, validation, and localStorage persistence
Workspace gains a Driver Logs tab (or Driver-Logs panel) with CSV/JSON upload UI and renders phase split + timing gaps + heatmap + state correlation
Inventory upload UI on Dashboard tab accepts CSV or paste
BOM tab supports row-level edit (qty, price override, already-owned)
Substitutions UI on BOM tab when catalog candidates exist

Chat tab renders suggestedActions as clickable chips that invoke the corresponding API
Workspace shell is responsive at 1024 px and 768 px breakpoints
Code tab has a Validate button that calls /code/validate and renders errors
Build tab renders the full LEGO-style step format from context.md
npm run lint and npm run build pass

tasks.name: integrate-mvp
tasks.scopedGoal:
Merge the backend and frontend branches into a single integrated deliverable branch, fix every conflict and seam, and prove the integrated app builds, lints, and serves.

Upstream handoffs you depend on:
- `backend-mvp` on branch `orch/blueprint-mvp/backend-mvp`
- `frontend-mvp` on branch `orch/blueprint-mvp/frontend-mvp`

(The orchestrator will inline both handoffs into your prompt; read them.)

Procedure:
1. Start from `main`. Merge `orch/blueprint-mvp/backend-mvp` first (`git merge --no-ff origin/orch/blueprint-mvp/backend-mvp`). Resolve any conflicts with intent (back to context.md, never invent API behavior).
2. Then merge `orch/blueprint-mvp/frontend-mvp` (`git merge --no-ff origin/orch/blueprint-mvp/frontend-mvp`). Resolve conflicts.
3. Reconcile the API contract between the two branches:
   - If the backend exposes fewer endpoints than the frontend calls, add the missing endpoint as a thin shim in `server/index.js` (or the relevant `server/lib/*.js` module) using existing helpers.
   - If the frontend reads a field the backend never returns, either add it as `null`/default on the backend or guard the frontend with an `?? defaultValue` fallback.
   - Re-shape only when the contract demands it. Do NOT rewrite either branch's intent.
4. Run and fix until green:
   - `npm install`
   - `npm run lint`
   - `npm run build`
   - `node --test server/__tests__/`
   - `node server/index.js &` in background; smoke `curl localhost:8787/api/health`; kill the server.
5. Manually smoke-test the user flow with the dev server: start project → wizard → workspace → upload manual → generate blueprint → BOM editable → driver-log upload → sponsor email. Capture a 30–60s screen recording of the end-to-end flow under `docs/screenshots/blueprint-mvp-demo.mp4` (or PNG screenshots if recording is unavailable) and reference it in the handoff. If the cloud env cannot record, take 3–6 screenshots at key transitions instead.
6. Update `context.md`'s 'Current Implementation Status' legend to reflect what now ships [x] vs still [~]/[ ]. Be honest; do not flip items you didn't actually deliver.
7. Update `README.md` with a 'Run the MVP' section: `npm install`, `npm run dev`, open http://localhost:5173, optional env vars (`VERTEX_AI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` if backend added them), and how to run the test suites.
8. Commit, push branch `orch/blueprint-mvp/integrate-mvp`, and open a draft PR against `main` titled 'Blueprint FTC MVP integration' with a body that lists what each upstream worker delivered and links to context.md acceptance criteria.

Quality floor (per orchestrate skill):
- No placeholder TODOs in merged code.
- No `throw new Error('not implemented')`.
- Lint + build green.
- Server boots without crashing.
- Frontend builds and the dev server serves the wizard at /.

Handoff branch: `orch/blueprint-mvp/integrate-mvp`. Open the PR.
acceptance: Both upstream branches merged into orch/blueprint-mvp/integrate-mvp

README.md has a 'Run the MVP' section

tasks.name: verify-mvp-acceptance
tasks.scopedGoal:
Verify the integrated Blueprint FTC MVP branch against the context.md Acceptance Criteria. Exercise the running app (start server + dev server). Do not implement features; only verify and record evidence.

For each item in the planner-level acceptance checklist and each context.md Acceptance Criterion, record concrete execution evidence:
- the exact command run or UI action taken
- the response or screenshot path
- a pass/fail verdict

Where a criterion is UI-only, capture a screenshot under `docs/screenshots/verification/` and reference it in the handoff.

For each context.md item under 'Acceptance Criteria' (Create a team profile, Upload manual, Enter budget, Generate three concepts, Select concept, Receive BOM with cost, Rules citations, Torque/RPM, CAD layout, Java code download, Build steps, Chatbot, Sponsor email, Driver logs upload+suggestions): produce one bullet under '## Findings' with a [x]/[ ] verdict and evidence.

Report the strongest verification value:
- `live-ui-verified` if you actually clicked through the UI and confirmed the flow.
- `unit-test-verified` if you only ran the test suites and inspected output.
- `type-check-only` if you only ran `npm run build`.
- `verifier-blocked` if the env prevented running the app (record why).
- `verifier-failed` if the integrated branch genuinely does not meet the criteria.
acceptance: ## Verification line set explicitly
Each context.md acceptance criterion has its own bullet under ## Findings with evidence
Execution section lists at least: npm run build, npm run lint, server boot + /api/health, dev server load, wizard step-through, driver-log upload, sponsor email
Screenshots (or recording) referenced for UI-only criteria
